title: S6: Layer/Filter Architecture — performance + toggle granularity
description:
Design spike to determine the right architecture for feature visibility management.

## The Question
QGIS uses ~100 vector layers. MapLibre has a fundamentally different model (sources + style layers + filters). What's the right architecture for:
- Toggling individual team members on/off
- Toggling breadcrumbs vs current positions
- Toggling marker categories (clues, hazards, IPP/LKP, casualties)
- Toggling individual search areas / drawings
- Performance with 30+ devices, 8-hour mission, 30s tracking interval (~28K breadcrumb points)

## Options to evaluate
1. **QGIS-style: many MapLibre layers** (1 per device, 1 per drawing)
2. **Few sources, filter-based visibility** (GeoJSON properties + setFilter)
3. **Hybrid: source-per-category, filter-per-item** (tracking source, markers source, drawings source)

## Performance test
- [ ] Generate synthetic data: 30 devices × 8 hours × 30s interval = 28,800 breadcrumb points
- [ ] Render all breadcrumbs in MapLibre — measure FPS
- [ ] Toggle visibility of individual devices — measure response time
- [ ] Compare: many-layers vs few-sources+filters approach
- [ ] Test on a mid-range laptop (not just M4 Max)

## UX test
- [ ] Prototype a filter panel (show/hide by person, by category)
- [ ] Compare with QGIS layer tree UX — is the filter panel simpler for non-tech users?

## Reference
Current plugin layer management:
~/Documents/Qgis/sartracker/controllers/layers_controller.py
~/Documents/Qgis/sartracker/layers/manager.py
~/Documents/Qgis/sartracker/layers/schema.py

## Decisions needed

- Autosave interval (60s?)
- Backup file location and naming
- Mission archive/export format

title: M11: QGIS replacement parity program
description:
Full replacement program for the legacy QGIS SAR Tracker plugin.

Program goals:
- preserve life-safety behaviour and data integrity
- replace all remaining plugin operational workflows
- explicitly verify parity with tests and operator-focused acceptance passes

comments.author: Donal
comments.text:
Completed a legacy tracking deep-dive and captured the findings in `docs/mock-traccar-server-spec.md`.

Key locked points from the plugin:
- Traccar HTTP parity depends on `GET /api/devices`, `GET /api/positions`, and per-device `GET /api/positions?deviceId&from&to`.
- The plugin does not aggressively clear the map on empty/bad polls; last-good cache and map continuity are part of the safety model.
- Current positions are labeled from device `name`; breadcrumbs are segmented on gaps >5 minutes.
- Layer visibility depends on active-device filtering (`online` visible, `offline` hidden from tracking layers, `unknown` visible only if recent).

The note also proposes the mock-server shape for `sartracker-web`:
- standalone local mock service under `tools/mock-traccar/`
- Glenagenty-area multi-device playback rather than a single-device stub
- staggered starts, offline/unknown devices, breadcrumb-gap scenario, outage scenario

Recommended follow-on: create a dedicated bead for mock Traccar server + fixture generation so parity/testing work can proceed without rediscovering plugin behavior.

Initial research/spec is complete.

comments.author: Donal
comments.text:
Completed a source-level fitness review of the current mock Traccar server.

Verdict:
- strong foundation for manual demos
- not yet fit as a high-confidence parity harness

Key findings:
- `/health` is documented as unauthenticated, but the router currently requires auth before the health route is reached
- `GET /api/positions` is documented as current positions for online devices, but the implementation returns last visible points for any routed device; in a late-scenario probe, Team Delta was `offline` in `/api/devices` and still present in `/api/positions`
- playback timestamp compression at speeds >1x prevents faithful testing of breadcrumb-gap segmentation and stale/unknown timing behavior
- route generation uses `Math.random()` for EOC/Medic jitter and speeds, so outputs are not deterministic across runs
- Team Delta's special `goUnknownAfterMs` modeling does not line up cleanly with its generated route end time

Recommendation before relying on this heavily:
1. make `/health` truly public
2. align current-position responses with roster visibility/status rules
3. make the fixtures deterministic
4. separate visual-speed playback from threshold-accurate playback more clearly

comments.author: Donal
comments.text: Completed 5 hardening fixes: (1) /health public, (2) offline devices excluded from /api/positions, (3) seeded PRNG for deterministic routes, (4) real-time timestamp spacing regardless of playback speed, (5) Team Delta goUnknownAfterMs aligned with route end. Added 16 unit tests. All verification green: lint, 320 unit tests, build, 78 E2E tests.

title: OpenTopoMap 'tiles failed to load' badge is too eager
description:
The map-health badge displays `OpenTopoMap degraded: Some tiles failed to load` even when the basemap is rendering correctly and most tiles are present. This was observed repeatedly during deep production validation on 2026-05-17 against build `0.1.0+SHA.840323E34498` at https://sartracker-web.vercel.app/?missionHarness=1.

Evidence:
- tmp/prod-validation-2026-05-17/01-initial-load.png — degraded badge visible at first paint, before any meaningful operator interaction.
- tmp/prod-validation-2026-05-17/05-measure.png — same degraded message persists during normal use even though the topographic basemap renders fine.

Why it matters:
This is a life-safety operator-trust signal. A "degraded" map message that fires at first paint or during normal operation teaches operators to ignore it, which defeats the point of having the badge at all. The badge should only fire when the basemap is genuinely degraded enough to affect operational decisions.

Suggested investigation:
- Look at src/components/map-status-badge.tsx and the map-health source it consumes.
- Identify what counts as a "tile failure" today (timeout? non-200? abort? cancellation when panning?).
- Check whether tile cancellation during pan/zoom is being miscategorised as a load failure.
- Decide a threshold (failure count, failure rate, or sustained failure window) below which the badge should stay silent.
- Add unit coverage for the new threshold logic.

Possibly related — but separate — concerns to keep in mind:
- This may also fire on hosted Vercel runs when the OpenTopoMap upstream is rate-limiting; if so, the message text should distinguish "rate-limited" from "tiles failed".
- Other basemaps (ESRI Topo, ESRI Satellite, OSM) may have different failure characteristics; the threshold should be per-basemap-aware if necessary.

Non-goals:
- Switching basemap providers.
- Adding tile retry logic (separate concern).

Affects shared app — the badge is shared between hosted browser and Tauri lanes. Hosted is where it was directly observed.

comments.author: Donal
comments.text: Closed 2026-05-17. Two surgical fixes. 1) Filter the maplibre 'error' channel to tile-level failures only via a new pure helper src/features/map/is-tile-error-event.ts — maplibre raises 'error' for many non-tile situations (style validation, missing layers, image-source errors, canvas-source errors) and the previous code counted all of them against the operator-trust budget. The helper checks for a non-null object 'tile' field on the event payload, which is the field maplibre populates for genuine tile fetch failures (per fire(new t.k(i,{tile:e}))). 2) Widened the tile-health-tracker default thresholds from 3-in-10s to 5-in-30s in src/lib/tile-health-tracker.ts — the previous defaults were too sensitive for the realistic OpenTopoMap rate-limit pattern at first paint plus a couple of normal-use blips. New tests: tests/unit/is-tile-error-event.test.ts (6 tests) pins that non-tile errors are rejected; tests/unit/tile-health-tracker.test.ts adds 4 tests pinning the new operator-trust defaults; existing 13 tests updated to pass options explicitly so they aren't coupled to the operator-facing defaults. Interactive Playwright sanity check at tmp/2xp-verification/ proves the operator-facing behavior end-to-end against a live dev server: initial badge 'OpenTopoMap basemap ready' (no false positive on first paint); 4 non-tile errors → still ready (filter works); 4 tile errors burst → still ready (under 5-error threshold); 5 tile errors burst → degraded (threshold honored); idle while errors are still in window → stays degraded (recovery is not premature). Evidence screenshots 01-04 saved under tmp/2xp-verification/.

title: M5: Mission UI — start/pause/resume/finish + timers
description:
Mission lifecycle controls and dual timer display.

## Deliverable
- Mission control panel: Start, Pause/Resume, Finish buttons (colour-coded, flashing when paused)
- Dual timers: Elapsed (total since start) + Active Search (excludes pauses)
- Mission name input on start
- Start offset: back-date mission start 0-48 hours (spinner, for delayed command post setup and testing)
- Finish confirmation dialog — data remains editable after finish
- Separate Finalize step to lock data read-only (reversible with admin)
- Auto-detect paused mission on restart → prompt Resume or Start Fresh
- Mission state persisted to SQLite

## Behaviour (from plugin investigation — cite: mission_controller.py, mission_lifecycle_controller.py)

### State Machine
IDLE → ACTIVE → PAUSED ↔ ACTIVE → FINISHED → IDLE
- No backwards from FINISHED. Must start a new mission.
- FINISHED auto-transitions to IDLE internally.

### Pause
- Active search timer stops. Elapsed timer keeps running.
- Tracking data still accepted (auto-refresh suppressed, manual refresh works)
- Markers and drawings CAN still be placed/edited
- UI: Pause button becomes Resume with flashing animation

### Finish
- Stops all timing, resets to IDLE
- Data remains EDITABLE — not read-only
- Dialog says: 'Keep all mission data editable. Use Finalize later to archive and lock.'
- Incoming tracking stops (is_active returns false)
- Saved pause state cleared

### Finalize (separate from Finish)
- Creates archive ZIP (GeoPackage snapshot + project + attachments)
- Marks mission read-only (all layers set read-only)
- Reversible with admin credentials (unlock feature)
- Only available after mission reaches IDLE

### Restart Recovery
- On startup: check for persisted pause state
- If found: show Resume/Start Fresh dialog (NOT auto-resume)
- Resume: restore mission name, timers, pause state
- Start Fresh: clear saved state

### Back-dated Start
- Range: 0-48 hours (changed from plugin's 5h to support testing scenarios)
- Breadcrumbs fetched from the back-dated time
- Use case: teams already moving before command post starts, or replaying test data

### Single Active Mission
- One active mission at a time
- Multiple can exist on disk
- Starting new mission while paused is blocked — must resume or finish first

## Dependencies
- M2 complete (panel sits alongside map)
- M3 complete (mission state persisted to SQLite)

## Spike reference
- ~/Documents/Qgis/sartracker/controllers/mission_lifecycle_controller.py
- ~/Documents/Qgis/sartracker/controllers/mission_controller.py
- Investigation output: /tmp/m5-lifecycle.md

comments.text: Research follow-up requested for M5. Please resolve these before implementation: 1) Canonical lifecycle model: should finished missions remain persisted as finished until explicitly finalized, or should the UI transition to idle while persistence still retains a finished state? We need one model that matches M3 archive behavior. 2) Finalize scope: is Finalize part of M5 or should M5 stop at start/pause/resume/finish and leave finalize/unlock to a later bead? 3) Persisted statuses: confirm the exact status set we want in v1 and whether idle is a persisted mission status or only a UI/app-shell state. 4) Finish behavior: after Finish, does all tracking stop immediately, or is manual review/fetch still allowed while data remains editable? 5) Pause behavior: does background polling continue while auto-refresh is suppressed, or does polling stop entirely until resume/manual refresh? 6) Start Fresh on restart: does it merely dismiss the paused mission prompt, create a new mission, or explicitly archive/clear anything from the paused mission? 7) Admin unlock: what does reversible with admin mean in a desktop app for v1: local passphrase, hidden operator action, role-gated setting, or defer entirely? 8) Timer/domain rules: confirm the exact formulas for elapsed vs active-search time, especially with back-dated starts and multiple pause/resume cycles. 9) UI recovery copy: lock the actual operator wording for Resume vs Start Fresh and for Finish vs Finalize confirmation. Use this research to rewrite the bead body so the lifecycle model is unambiguous before coding starts.

title: R4: Surface lifecycle backup failures non-dismissably
description: Multi-agent S1/S2 review findings C2/H2: lifecycle transitions commit SQLite state before sync_backup runs, and forced sync after finish/finalize/unlock may conflict with active-mission audit assumptions. Decide and implement the correct lifecycle backup contract, then surface failures in a way operators cannot miss.
acceptance_criteria: The sync_backup contract after start/pause/resume/finish/finalize/unlock is verified against Rust semantics and documented; lifecycle backup failure cannot be silently hidden behind only a small chip; tests cover the chosen contract and failure UI; operator manual explains what to do when lifecycle backup fails.

comments.author: Donal
comments.text: Implemented and verified R4. Lifecycle-forced autosave failures now produce a persistent non-dismissible alert below the mast, including the lifecycle reason and operator instructions. Backend contract is covered by a Rust test: sync_backup succeeds after finish/finalize while backup audit events remain active-mission-only. Operator manual and handoff/workplan updated. Verification passed: npm run lint; npm run test -- --run tests/unit/runtime-boot-gate.test.ts; npm run test:e2e -- --project=chromium tests/e2e/map.spec.ts; npm run test -- --run; npm run build; npm run test:backend; npm run test:e2e -- --project=chromium.

comments.author: Donal
comments.text:
Claude quick review follow-up, captured as non-blocking context:

- `mission-autosave.ts` intentionally uses `requireActiveMission: false` for lifecycle `requestSync(...)` calls. After `finishMission`, runtime `currentMission` is null, but `sync_backup` should still run so the post-finish backup mirror is written. Rust owns the invariant that no active mission means no backup audit row. Next Tauri smoke build should do a one-time confirmation that post-finish `sync_backup` does not log a misleading audit row.
- `enqueueAutosave` short-circuits with `Promise.resolve()` when stopped and does not `markSyncFailed`. If a forced lifecycle sync arrives during teardown, it silently no-ops. This is edge-case-only and not a current code defect; revisit if teardown/lifecycle sequencing changes.

title: M4: Tracking — Traccar HTTP polling + map rendering
description:
Real-time GPS tracking from Traccar server displayed on the map.

## Deliverable
- TraccarClient: authenticate, fetch devices, fetch positions, fetch breadcrumbs
- PollingManager: configurable interval, retry with exponential backoff
- Device markers on the map (coloured per device)
- Breadcrumb trails per device (coloured lines)
- Stale device detection (>5 min no update → visual indicator)
- Last-good cache (serve last known positions when server unreachable)
- Positions persisted to SQLite via MissionStore

## Decisions (locked)
- HTTP polling for v1, not WebSocket (decided during spike research)
- Polling interval: 30s default, configurable
- Mock auth for tests; real Traccar credentials from Eamonn when available

## Decisions (open)
- Device colour assignment strategy (random? sequential? user-configurable?)
- Breadcrumb trail styling (dots vs line vs both)

## Dependencies
- M2 complete (map must be rendering)
- M3 complete (persistence for storing positions)

## Spike reference
- spikes/S7-traccar-integration/ — TraccarClient, PollingManager, connection test

title: R2: Replace autosave wall-clock stale detection
description: Multi-agent S1/S2 review finding C1/M7: autosave stale detection subtracts Date.now from lastSuccessAt, creating false stale warnings after laptop sleep, browser timer suspension, or clock jumps. Replace with monotonic/tick-driven elapsed tracking and add hysteresis or debounce where needed.
acceptance_criteria: Autosave stale warnings are based on monotonic elapsed/tick state rather than wall-clock subtraction; fake-timer tests simulate sleep/clock jumps and do not produce false positives; real stale/failing states still surface clearly.

comments.author: Donal
comments.text: Implemented R2. Autosave stale warnings now use observed command-mast tick elapsed time rather than wall-clock subtraction, so clock jumps or laptop sleep do not immediately produce false stale warnings. Verification passed: npm run lint; npm run test -- --run tests/unit/mission-autosave.test.ts tests/unit/runtime-boot-gate.test.ts; npm run test -- --run tests/unit/browser-harness-store.test.ts after one known quota-test timeout under concurrent load; npm run test -- --run; npm run build; npm run test:backend; npm run test:e2e -- --project=chromium.

title: M3: Persistence — SQLite mission store
description:
Crash-safe mission persistence via SQLite behind Tauri commands.

## Deliverable
- SQLite database with WAL mode via Tauri SQL plugin
- Schema: missions, devices, positions, markers, drawings, mission_events
- MissionStore API exposed via Tauri commands (frontend never touches DB directly)
- Schema versioning with migrations
- Autosave (configurable interval, non-blocking)
- Backup rotation (keep last 3)
- Crash recovery: kill app → reopen → data intact

## Decisions (locked)
- Tauri SQL plugin (@tauri-apps/plugin-sql) with sqlx backend (decided M1 research)
- Frontend calls MissionStore methods, never raw SQL
- WAL mode for crash safety
- Autosave interval: 60s default

## Dependencies
- M1 complete (Tauri must be running)

## Spike reference
- spikes/S5-persistence/ — SQLite schema, MissionStore class, migration pattern, crash recovery tests

title: S3: Drawing Tools — Terra Draw + Turf.js SAR parity
description:
Spike to validate Terra Draw + Turf.js can replicate all SAR-specific drawing tools from the QGIS plugin.

## Goal
Prove all 8 drawing modes work with correct geometry and SAR metadata support.

## Tasks
- [ ] Build MapLibre + Terra Draw prototype
- [ ] Implement range rings (n concentric circles at custom radii) via Turf.js
- [ ] Implement LPB probability rings (25/50/75/95%) via Turf.js
- [ ] Implement bearing lines (true + magnetic) with correct endpoint
- [ ] Implement search sectors (pie-slice) via Terra Draw sector mode
- [ ] Implement search area polygons with POA/team/status metadata
- [ ] Verify select + edit + delete for all feature types
- [ ] Playwright test: verify geometry matches plugin output

## Risk: MEDIUM
If Terra Draw can't handle SAR geometry, we build custom drawing modes — more work but not fatal.

comments.author: Donal
comments.text:
A3.5 implementation 2026-05-16:

Audit: ran the dev server at http://127.0.0.1:1420/?missionHarness=1 and captured before/after screenshots in tmp/contrast-audit/{before,after}/.

Findings:
- text-stone-500 was overused for body labels (~99 occurrences). On the dark panel background that is below WCAG AA contrast.
- Faded amber section subtitles (text-amber-300/80, /90) read as washed-out branding rather than strong section labels.
- Status pills (mission phase, tracking mode) used per-color borders without consistent semantics.
- Disabled-state opacity:20 made disabled controls invisible.
- Map overlay chrome borders (stone-line/0.17) were thin against bright satellite tiles.
- Autosave warning chip rendered at text-[9px] – essentially invisible.
- Settings descriptions used the same washed-out grey as field hints.

Changes:
- index.css: added --sar-text-strong, --sar-text-muted (#c2bdb5 ≥ AA on panels), --sar-text-dim tokens; added .sar-helper-text and .sar-meta-label utilities.
- index.css: introduced semantic chips (.sar-status-chip-success/warning/danger/neutral), .sar-inline-critical/.sar-inline-success, .sar-map-chip.
- index.css: strengthened panel/module/readout borders for hierarchy; added :focus-visible amber outline on all primary buttons; added .sar-button :hover:not(:disabled), proper :disabled treatments (opacity 0.4, saturate 0.6).
- index.css: lifted control-dock and instrument-strip borders/shadows for satellite-tile readability; bumped placeholder color contrast.
- App.tsx: replaced phase pill hand-rolled classes with sar-status-chip-* tokens; lifted version, mission subline, system-status-detail, TopReadout label color; replaced text-[9px] autosave warning with a sar-status-chip-warning chip; tightened the hosted-browser banner border/bg; promoted the lifecycle backup failure banner to sar-inline-critical; switched sidebar tabs to sar-tab-inactive.
- mission-control-panel: lifted lifecycle subtitle, phase span, label colors, governance subtitle, recovery dialog amber, and amber-100/80 -> amber-100.
- tracking-status-panel: replaced the inline mode chip with sar-status-chip-* tokens; lifted column header colors; emphasized telemetry warning border-l-4 with brighter amber-100 / emerald-200 readouts.
- coordinate-bar: switched instrument labels to sar-meta-label; lifted secondary coord/CRS readouts to text-stone-100.
- basemap-switcher: bumped border + label contrasts; lifted active/inactive button hover states for amber affordance; brightened coverage detail text.
- drawing-toolbar: stronger active and inactive button states; cyan-tinted Measure active state (kept it visually distinct from drawing tools); bumped collapsed badge text.
- layer-filter-panel: lifted subtitle, expand chevron, child count, kind label, detail row label, empty-state border/text.
- marker-at-grid-panel: switched select/input to sar-input; bumped subtitle; switched error to sar-inline-critical.
- measurement-panel: replaced bespoke stone-800 chrome with sar-module + sar-readout; brighter header colors; cyan affordance kept.
- gpx-import-panel + helicopter-panel: bumped sub-text contrast and helicopter SET/EMPTY chip color.
- devices-workspace + diagnostics-workspace: lifted column headers, last-success label, status mode colors, empty-state, detail label/value contrasts (all to ≥AA).
- map-status-badge + offline-map-readiness-badge: stronger borders against bright basemap tiles; opacity 0.85 -> 0.95 for detail strings.
- workspace-overlay header: stronger Esc kbd; full-opacity subtitle.
- marker-dialog: helper text uses sar-helper-text; LPB percentile labels brightened.
- focus-mode-sidebar: removed faded amber subtitle.

Verification:
- npm run lint: clean.
- npx tsc --noEmit: clean.
- npm run build: vite production build clean (<7.95kB css gz, no bundle-size regressions).
- npm run test: 88 files / 435 tests passed.
- npm run test:e2e: 80 chromium + 27 visual tests passed (107 total).
- npm run test:backend: 38 Rust tests passed.
- npm run test:all: green end-to-end.
- Browser-backed verification: ran dev server and captured idle, tracking, tools, layers, settings, diagnostics, narrow viewport (1024 + 900), satellite basemap, active mission, drawing toolbar expanded, marker at GR error/dialog, coordinate converter. Evidence in tmp/contrast-audit/after/.

No workflow, lifecycle, persistence, coordinate, or tracking semantics were changed. No tests were skipped or weakened.

comments.author: Donal
comments.text:
RESEARCH COMPLETE — all 3 open questions resolved (2026-04-09 by Forge)

## 1. Magnetic Declination — LOCKED: fixed -4.5° for v1

The plugin hardcodes -4.5° at bearing_tool.py:45 (MAGNETIC_DECLINATION_IRELAND = -4.5).
Current actual value for Kerry (Carrauntoohil area) is approximately -4° to -5° depending on exact location.
The plugin stores TRUE bearing and converts for display only.

Conversion rules:
- True → Magnetic: magnetic = true + (-4.5) = true - 4.5°
- Magnetic → True: true = magnetic - (-4.5) = magnetic + 4.5°
- Both normalize to 0-360°

Decision: keep fixed -4.5° for v1. Dynamic WMM is Phase 3. Add a note in settings that this is approximate.

## 2. LPB Distances — LOCKED: from Koester/NASAR (already in plugin)

Source: 'Lost Person Behavior' by Robert Koester, NASAR guidelines.
Defined in plugin at utils/lpb_statistics.py:18-82.

| Category | 25% | 50% | 75% | 95% |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Child (1-3) | 100m | 300m | 700m | 1900m |
| Child (4-6) | 200m | 500m | 1100m | 2400m |
| Child (7-12) | 500m | 1300m | 2500m | 3800m |
| Hiker | 800m | 2000m | 4000m | 8000m |
| Hunter | 1200m | 3000m | 5500m | 10000m |
| Elderly | 200m | 500m | 1200m | 2500m |
| Dementia | 100m | 300m | 800m | 2000m |
| Despondent | 200m | 500m | 1500m | 3000m |
| Autistic | 200m | 600m | 1200m | 2000m |

Ring colours: 25%=green, 50%=yellow, 75%=orange, 95%=red.

## 3. Tool Activation UX — LOCKED: one-active-at-a-time, matches plugin

- Single toolbar, one active tool at a time
- Click tool button to arm it
- ESC cancels current drawing
- Tools auto-deactivate after creating a feature (one-shot)
- Right-click finishes multi-point tools (line, polygon)
- Activating another tool deactivates the current one
- Default returns to pan/select mode after deactivation
- Active tool indicator shown in UI ('Active: Bearing', etc.)

All three questions are now locked. M8 implementation readiness: 5/5.

title: Visual review prompts drifted from current UI for 5 specs
description:
The V2 visual review automation (sartracker-web-n9i) caught five visual specs whose verificationPrompt no longer matches the captured screenshot. These are spec drift, not app bugs: the prompts ask for text/elements that are not in the captured frame.

Affected specs:
- mission-active-state — prompt asks for mission name 'RIDGE SEARCH ALPHA' but the captureElementAndRegister scope is 'mission-control', which excludes the mast where the name actually renders.
- mission-backdated-offset — same pattern; mission name 'DELAYED START' is outside the captured element.
- mission-paused-state — same pattern; mission name 'PAUSE TEST MISSION' is outside the captured element.
- shell-drawing-toolbar — prompt asks for 'ACTIVE: SELECT' indicator at top, but actual UI shows 'MAP TOOLS / MISSION REQUIRED'.
- drawing-multiple-on-map — prompt asks for search area polygon, bearing line, and 'Command Post' text label visible; only range rings render in the screenshot.

For each spec, decide whether to:
- expand the captured element (full page or wider scope) so the verification target is in frame, OR
- update the verificationPrompt to describe what the captured element actually contains.

This was discovered by running 'npm run visual:review' against the current visual manifest. See test-results/visual-verification/reports/visual-review-2026-05-17T15-47-49Z.json.

title: M2: Map — MapLibre + basemaps + coordinate display
description:
Map rendering with basemap switching and live coordinate bar.

## Deliverable
- MapLibre GL JS rendering in the Tauri window via raw useRef
- Basemap switcher with 4 sources (see locked decisions below)
- Selected basemap persisted to localStorage
- Coordinate bar showing WGS84 + Irish Grid (TM65) on mouse move
- Map centered on Kerry (51.97, -9.70), default zoom 12
- Service Worker tile caching for offline resilience

## Decisions (locked)
- Raw MapLibre via useRef — no react-map-gl (decided M1 research)
- proj4js for TM65 conversion using TOWGS84 params from S2 spike
- Service Worker for tile caching (Tauri filesystem for GeoPackage deferred)

### Basemap URLs (locked)
1. **OpenTopoMap (default):** `https://tile.opentopomap.org/{z}/{x}/{y}.png` — attribution: © OpenTopoMap contributors
2. **ESRI World Topo:** `https://services.arcgisonline.com/arcgis/rest/services/World_Topo_Map/MapServer/tile/{z}/{y}/{x}` — attribution: © Esri — NOTE: tile order is {z}/{y}/{x}
3. **OpenStreetMap:** `https://tile.openstreetmap.org/{z}/{x}/{y}.png` — attribution: © OpenStreetMap contributors
4. **ESRI Satellite:** `https://services.arcgisonline.com/arcgis/rest/services/World_Imagery/MapServer/tile/{z}/{y}/{x}` — attribution: © Esri — NOTE: tile order is {z}/{y}/{x}

### Coordinate bar format (locked)
Match the existing QGIS plugin display conventions:
- WGS84: 6 decimal places with directional suffix — e.g. `52.274681°N, 9.530912°W`
- TM65 Irish Grid Reference: 5-digit format — e.g. `V 80245 84452`
- Display side by side: `52.274681°N, 9.530912°W  |  V 80245 84452`

### Offline resilience promise for v1 (locked)
- Service Worker caches tiles as viewed — previously viewed tiles available offline
- No pre-cache or bulk tile download in v1
- If user pans to an unvisited area while offline, tiles will be blank
- GeoPackage-based offline maps deferred until file arrives from Eamonn

## Dependencies
- M1 complete (scaffold running — ✅ done)

## Open items (non-blocking)
- GeoPackage offline tile loading deferred until KMRT_package.gpkg arrives

## Spike reference
- spikes/S1-osi-maps/ — basemap switcher, caching, coordinate bar (29 tests)
- spikes/S2-irish-grid/ — proj4js coordinate conversion (201 tests)

title: Runtime tracking adapter does not start on desktop boot despite saved provider with auto-connect
description:
Discovered during B3 (sartracker-web-ppr) first internal Tauri smoke build on 0.1.0+sha.e7ead2eb093a (commit e7ead2eb093a).

Steps:
1. Open Settings, configure provider type: traccar_http, URL: http://kmrtsar.ddns.net:8082, auth: basic, user: apiuser, password: apiuser, auto-connect: on. Save.
2. Connection test in Settings reports the connection succeeds.
3. After save, observe the System Status / right-rail tracking area.

Expected: tracking runtime should be in a connecting/live/idle-with-provider state, with warnings only when something genuinely went wrong.

Actual: System Status warning area shows 'Tracking is not configured' AND 'Tracking provider is configured but runtime tracking is not connected' simultaneously. Diagnostics report agrees:
- [configuration] provider type: traccar_http, provider url: http://kmrtsar.ddns.net:8082, auto connect: yes, runtime tracking configured: no
- [tracking] tracking mode: idle, last success: never, devices/positions/breadcrumbs in snapshot: 0
- [warnings] Tracking is not configured. AND Tracking provider is configured but runtime tracking is not connected.

That is contradictory: the provider IS configured (provider type/URL/auth all populated), but 'runtime tracking configured' is no. Either the runtime adapter is failing to bootstrap from saved settings, or the warning text is a false alarm and only the second message should appear.

Likely areas to inspect:
- src/features/tracking/** for the bootstrap path between SettingsStore -> tracking runtime.
- src/infrastructure/tracking/* and src-tauri/src/tracking_cache.rs for runtime registration.
- src-tauri/src/settings.rs builds_runtime_bootstrap_without_connecting_when_auto_connect_is_disabled — there is unit coverage for the disabled path; consider equivalent coverage for the enabled path on real desktop boot.

Severity: P1 not P0 because it does not corrupt mission data, but it is operator-confusing on a life-safety app and undermines trust in the warning surface.

Evidence: tmp/beta-artifacts/smoke/02-mission-started-tracking-warnings.png and the diagnostics report referenced in sartracker-web-zl4.
close_reason: Fixed in 000f7d1 (keyring platform features) + 603771f (ATS exception). Categorically proven end-to-end on packaged 0.1.0 .app: tracking-cache.json populated with 18 real devices, SQLite devices table populated, mast shows 'DEVICES ONLINE 18 / ONLINE'. Tests added across both layers (Rust unit, Rust integration with real macOS keychain probe, TS unit) to pin the regressions.

comments.author: Donal
comments.text:
Sharpened understanding after cross-checking the on-disk state: this is not a 'warning text is wrong' bug, the runtime really is dead on arrival on desktop. SQLite confirms: devices=0 rows, positions=0 rows. No tracking-cache file exists. Diagnostics report shows consecutive_failures=0 and last_success=never, which means the poller never executed a single HTTP request — the Settings 'Test Connection' button reached the server (separate code path) but the always-on runtime poller never started.

Reframed root cause hypothesis: the desktop runtime bootstrap is not consuming the saved provider settings + auto-connect flag at app start. The browser/hosted mode appears to work (operators have been using it), so this is desktop-specific.

Likely areas to inspect first:
- src-tauri/src/settings.rs builds_runtime_bootstrap_without_connecting_when_auto_connect_is_disabled is the only documented bootstrap unit test; the symmetric 'auto-connect enabled' path may be missing or wired to a no-op.
- src/lib/app-runtime-controller.ts and src/features/tracking/** for the boot-time call into the tracking runtime on Tauri vs. browser.
- Whether the runtime is gated on an active mission AND auto-connect — the smoke run had an active mission so that gate alone shouldn't be the cause.

Severity revisited: still P1 not P0 because the Settings test-connection probe still works (operators can prove the upstream is reachable) and no mission data is corrupted. But for a SAR app, an operator-visible 'tracking is on, configured, auto-connect on' state with no actual telemetry is dangerous, and this likely matters at least as much as the lifecycle quit bug for real-world use.

comments.author: Donal
comments.text:
VERDICT: real bug, fixed and categorically proven end-to-end.

Root cause (proven): src-tauri/Cargo.toml had keyring = '3.6.3' with no platform features. Per the keyring v3 docs, that means the crate falls back to a process-local mock backend. set_password returned Ok and round-trips in-process via the mock, but nothing ever reached the OS Keychain. On the next boot the mock store was empty, so runtime_bootstrap (settings.rs:312) saw secret.is_none(), returned tracking_config: None, and the renderer's startTrackingRuntime (start-tracking-runtime.ts:105) took the early-return branch — no poller was ever created. That is exactly the failure mode the operator observed: 'Tracking is not configured / Tracking provider is configured but runtime tracking is not connected', last_success: never, devices: 0, positions: 0.

Cargo.lock confirmed the diagnosis: the resolved keyring 3.6.3 had only log + zeroize as deps, no security-framework, no core-foundation. The macOS Keychain backend was not compiled in.

Fix in 000f7d1: Cargo.toml -> keyring = { version = '3.6.3', features = ['apple-native', 'windows-native', 'sync-secret-service'] }. Cross-platform, no machine-specific hack. Pulls in security-framework on macOS, wincred on Windows, dbus-secret-service on Linux.

A second related bug surfaced and was fixed in 603771f: macOS App Transport Security blocked the renderer's WKWebView fetch() to plain http:// Traccar URLs. Fix: add src-tauri/Info.plist with NSAllowsArbitraryLoads = true. Auto-discovered by the Tauri bundler and merged into the bundled Info.plist (verified via PlistBuddy).

Tests added (all in main / 000f7d1):
- src-tauri/src/settings.rs: builds_runtime_bootstrap_with_traccar_config_when_auto_connect_is_enabled_and_secret_present (positive symmetric of the existing _disabled_ test)
- src-tauri/src/settings.rs: keyring_secret_store_writes_to_real_macos_keychain (#[ignore]'d, #[cfg(target_os = 'macos')]). Writes via KeyringSecretStore then verifies via 'security find-generic-password' in a SEPARATE process. Failed before the fix with the exact 'item could not be found' error; passes after.
- src-tauri/src/persistence.rs: active_mission_survives_process_restart_without_explicit_finish (related zl4 regression).
- tests/unit/start-tracking-runtime.test.ts: 'starts the poller when the runtime config is present' — pins that poller.start() is called when tracking_config is non-null.

Live end-to-end proof on packaged .app build 0.1.0+sha.603771f65431:
- Seeded matching settings.json + keychain entry + active mission
- Launched the packaged .app
- Operator clicked Resume on the recovery dialog (one click, only place that genuinely needs UI)
- 30 seconds later: tracking-cache.json grew from 85 bytes (empty placeholder) to 6,231 bytes containing real device data ('Aiden Forde', 'Bertie', 'Cathal Cudden', etc.)
- SQLite devices table populated with 18 rows, positions table with 14 rows (matches upstream Traccar's 19 devices, 1 deduped on normalisation as expected)
- Live screenshot of the running app showed: mast 'DEVICES ONLINE 18', System Status 'ONLINE' (green), 'Last success' populated, 'Telemetry stream healthy', 14 trackpoints rendered on the map around the Killarney/Kerry area, helicopters Rescue 116 and Rescue 119 listed

Evidence files: tmp/beta-artifacts/smoke-rerun/01..09*.png, tmp/beta-artifacts/smoke-rerun/devices.tsv, tmp/beta-artifacts/verify-0.1.0-sha.603771f65431-*.json.

title: B6: GPX and drawing hit-test hardening
description:
Make GPX and drawing/marker map-click hit-test priority explicit and testable.

CURRENT BUGS / AMBIGUITIES (verified by reading src):
- src/features/map/use-map-drawing-interactions.ts and src/features/map/use-map-marker-interactions.ts both register window-level capture-phase 'click' handlers. The drawing handler currently runs first and calls event.stopImmediatePropagation() when it selects a drawing. RESULT: a marker stacked inside a polygon (e.g. LKP marker placed inside a search-area) is silently swallowed by the polygon — the polygon edit dialog opens instead of the marker dialog. This is the headline bug.
- Hit-test priority is implicit (registration order) and undocumented. There is no single place that names the rule.
- GPX tracks have zero hit-testing — operators cannot select or identify them, and the rule for clicks-near-a-GPX-line is not explicit.

PLAN (TDD):
1. Add src/features/map/map-click-target-resolver.ts — pure fn returning { kind: 'marker'|'drawing'|'empty', id } with documented priority marker > drawing > empty. Also exposes a soft signal gpxNearbyImportId for future surface work, but does not change current click outcome on bare GPX hits.
2. Add src/features/gpx/gpx-hit-testing.ts — pure fn findNearestGpxImportId for LineString tracks (reuses the same projected-segment-distance math as drawing-hit-testing).
3. Refactor useMapMarkerInteractions and useMapDrawingInteractions to consult the resolver. Each hook acts only when its kind wins; no more event.stopImmediatePropagation race.

title: M3: Persistence — SQLite mission store
description:
Crash-safe mission persistence via SQLite behind Tauri commands.

## Deliverable
- SQLite database with WAL mode via Tauri SQL plugin
- MissionStore API exposed via Tauri commands (frontend never touches DB directly)
- Schema versioning with migrations
- Autosave (default 60s, configurable)
- Backup: single mirror copy with atomic write (temp+rename)
- Crash recovery: detect paused mission on restart, prompt user
- Archive: ZIP export of completed missions

## Schema (from plugin investigation — cite: layers/schema.py v4)

### missions
id, name, status (idle/active/paused/finished/finalized), start_time, pause_time, finish_time, paused_seconds, notes, schema_version

### devices
id, mission_id, device_id, name, color, last_seen, status (online/offline/unknown)

### positions
id, mission_id, device_id, name, lat, lon, altitude, speed, battery, accuracy, source, timestamp, data_origin (live/cache)

### markers (4 types, common + type-specific fields)
**Common:** id, mission_id, type, name, description, lat, lon, irish_grid_e, irish_grid_n, created_at, updated_at, display_order
**IPP/LKP:** subject_category
**Clue:** clue_type, confidence, found_by
**Hazard:** hazard_type, severity
**Casualty:** condition, treatment, evacuation_priority, found_by

### drawings (6 types)
**Lines:** id, mission_id, name, description, color, width, distance_m, temporary_measure, display_order
**Search Areas:** id, mission_id, name, team, status, priority, area_sqkm, POA, POD, terrain, search_method, color, start_time, end_time, notes, display_order
**Range Rings:** id, mission_id, name, center_lat, center_lon, radius_m, label, color, lpb_category, percentile, display_order
**Bearing Lines:** id, mission_id, name, origin_lat, origin_lon, bearing, distance_m, label, color, display_order
**Search Sectors:** id, mission_id, name, center_lat, center_lon, start_bearing, end_bearing, radius_m, arc_length_deg, area_sqkm, priority, color, display_order
**Text Labels:** id, mission_id, text, lat, lon, font_size, color, rotation, display_order

### mission_events
id, mission_id, event_type, timestamp, details_json

## Persistence Rules (from investigation)
- Feature edits (markers, drawings, positions): saved immediately
- Mission state transitions: saved immediately
- Project-level save (GeoPackage integrity check + backup sync): on autosave timer
- Backup: single mirror, atomic write via temp+rename, no rotation
- Archive: ZIP with GeoPackage snapshot + attachments, validated after creation

## Dependencies
- M1 complete (Tauri running)

## Spike reference
- spikes/S5-persistence/ — MissionStore prototype, schema, migrations
- Investigation output: /tmp/m3-persistence.md

description:

comments.author: Donal
comments.text:
V2 implementation complete locally on master.

Architecture:
- build/visual-review-lib.js — pure shaping/parsing helpers; 44 unit tests in tests/unit/visual-review-lib.test.ts.
- scripts/visual-review.mjs — runner; spawns one `claude --print --output-format json` subprocess per manifest entry.
- npm run visual:review — entry point. Flags: --only, --no-cache, --dry-run, --fail-on, --concurrency, --model, --manifest-dir, --report-dir.

Reviewer subprocess:
- Uses `claude --print --output-format json --model opus --add-dir <screenshot-dir> --allowedTools Read --no-session-persistence --permission-mode bypassPermissions`.
- The Claude envelope `{type:"result","result":"..."}` is parsed first to detect is_error and unwrap the assistant text. The unwrapped text is then run through parseModelReply, which tolerates fenced or embedded JSON but rejects replies with no recognisable verdict.

Severity gating:
- Default --fail-on=high; failOn=critical and failOn=medium also supported.
- failOn=high blocks on any high-or-critical FAIL but not on medium.
- Reviewer errors always block (exit 2) regardless of failOn.

Cache:
- Keyed by sha256 of (model + verificationPrompt + severity + screenshot bytes).
- Stored under test-results/visual-verification/.cache/<key>.json.
- --no-cache bypasses both reads and writes.

Per-entry results: test-results/visual-verification/results/<testId>.review.json.
Aggregate report: test-results/visual-verification/reports/visual-review-<UTC>.json.

Verification (red-then-green where applicable):
- New tests/unit/visual-review-lib.test.ts failed red against the missing build/visual-review-lib.js, then 44/44 pass after implementation.
- Full unit suite: 96 files, 520 tests pass (was 476 before V2; added 44).

title: Route renderer Traccar fetch through Rust reqwest to remove ATS blanket exception
description:
Follow-up to el9/B3. The current 0.1.0 internal-beta lane uses NSAppTransportSecurity -> NSAllowsArbitraryLoads = true (src-tauri/Info.plist) so the renderer's WKWebView fetch() can reach team-hosted Traccar over plain HTTP. That is acceptable for an unsigned/un-notarised internal beta but is App Store-incompatible and a wider-than-needed exception.

Long-term replacement: route the renderer's Traccar HTTP calls through a new Tauri command backed by Rust reqwest (already used for the Settings test-connection path). reqwest bypasses macOS ATS entirely. The renderer would invoke this command via @tauri-apps/api invoke() instead of using globalThis.fetch directly. The dev-server / hosted-browser path keeps using fetch(), so the implementation should be conditional on tauri runtime detection (already used elsewhere in src/features/runtime/bootstrap-app-runtime.ts).

Acceptance:
- Tauri command e.g. tauri-traccar-fetch that proxies requests with full Basic-auth/cookie semantics matching traccar-client.ts.
- traccar-client.ts swaps fetchFn for the Tauri-aware variant on desktop.
- src-tauri/Info.plist NSAllowsArbitraryLoads removed (or scoped to NSExceptionDomains for any remaining HTTPS-only requirement).
- Existing E2E and unit tests still green.
- Manual desktop verification: tracking still polls and devices still appear with NSAllowsArbitraryLoads removed.

Severity: P2. Not blocking the internal beta lane. Should land before any signed/notarised public distribution.
close_reason: Implemented and live-smoked: desktop Traccar polling routes through Rust reqwest, ATS blanket plist removed, packaged app polls real Traccar successfully after mission resume/keychain access.

title: M4: Tracking — Traccar HTTP polling + map rendering
description:
Real-time GPS tracking from Traccar server displayed on the map.

## Deliverable
- TraccarClient: authenticate, fetch devices, fetch positions, fetch breadcrumbs
- PollingManager: configurable interval (30s default), retry with exponential backoff
- Device markers on map (coloured circles per device)
- Breadcrumb trails per device (dotted lines, same colour as device)
- Stale device detection + visual indicator
- Last-good cache with offline mode warning
- Incremental breadcrumb fetching (per-device timestamp tracking)
- Positions persisted to SQLite via MissionStore

## Decisions (locked)
- HTTP polling for v1 (30s default, configurable)
- Mock auth for tests; real credentials from Eamonn when available
- Device colours: MD5 hash of device_id, deterministic, 50-255 range, collision-resistant
- Breadcrumb trails: dotted lines (width 1.5), current positions: circle markers (size 5)
- Stale device: 1hr visibility threshold, 5min cache TTL, yellow indicator
- Last-good cache: full per-device snapshots + breadcrumbs, JSON on disk, 4hr max age, atomic writes
- Traccar unreachable: serve cached data, OFFLINE MODE warning, never clear map, CONNECTION RESTORED on recovery
- Incremental breadcrumbs: per-device timestamp tracking, dedup by (device_id, timestamp), 100K max, FIFO eviction
- JSON cache is transport-only; SQLite (MissionStore) is always authoritative
- Canonical test fixtures to be stored in tests/fixtures/

## Traccar API Payloads (from plugin investigation)

### Device response (/api/devices)
Required: id (int)
Optional: name (fallback 'Device {id}'), status, lastUpdate, uniqueId, category

### Position response (/api/positions)
Required: deviceId (int), latitude (float), longitude (float), one of fixTime/serverTime/deviceTime
Optional: id, altitude, speed, valid, attributes.batteryLevel

### Normalisation: API → internal
deviceId → device_id (string), latitude → lat, longitude → lon, fixTime → ts (ISO8601 UTC), altitude → altitude, speed → speed, attributes.batteryLevel → battery
Post-annotation: data_origin (live/cache), cache_age_seconds, device_cache_stale

## Dependencies
- M2 complete ✅
- M3 complete ✅

## Spike reference
- spikes/S7-traccar-integration/
- Investigation: /tmp/m4-fixtures.md

comments.author: Donal
comments.text: Implemented M4 tracking end-to-end. Added Traccar client, polling manager, last-good cache + Tauri cache adapter, runtime orchestration, map overlay sync, stale/offline/recovery behavior, and mission-store persistence wiring. Added canonical fixtures plus unit/integration/E2E coverage. Verification: npm run test, npm run lint, npm run build, npm run test:e2e, cargo check, cargo test all green. Remaining non-code follow-up is live validation against real Traccar credentials when available.

title: M4: Tracking — Traccar HTTP polling + map rendering
description:
Real-time GPS tracking from Traccar server displayed on the map.

## Deliverable
- TraccarClient: authenticate, fetch devices, fetch positions, fetch breadcrumbs
- PollingManager: configurable interval (30s default), retry with exponential backoff
- Device markers on map (coloured circles per device)
- Breadcrumb trails per device (dotted lines, same colour as device)
- Stale device detection + visual indicator
- Last-good cache with offline mode warning
- Incremental breadcrumb fetching (per-device timestamp tracking)
- Positions persisted to SQLite via MissionStore

## Behaviour (from plugin investigation — cite: traccar_http.py, provider_controller.py, tracking_manager.py)

### Device Colours
- MD5 hash of device_id → deterministic, persistent, collision-resistant
- Range: 50-255 per channel (avoids dark/invisible)
- Collision check: if within 30 units on all channels, shift using second hash half
- NOT user-configurable in v1

### Breadcrumb Styling
- Dotted lines (line_style: dot, width: 1.5, round join/cap)
- Current positions: circle markers (size 5)
- Trails segmented on time gaps (5 min default)

### Stale Device
- Visibility threshold: 1 hour (device hidden from active list if no update for 1hr)
- Cache TTL: 5 minutes (provider-level)
- Visual: green (online), red (offline), yellow (unknown/stale)

### Last-Good Cache
- Full per-device position snapshots + breadcrumbs
- Stored as JSON on disk, atomic writes (temp+rename+fsync)
- Max age: 4 hours
- Individual position validation (bad entries dropped, not whole cache)

### Traccar Unreachable
- Serve cached data with OFFLINE MODE warning
- NEVER clear existing map data on empty/failed response
- Track consecutive failures + outage duration
- Show CONNECTION RESTORED notification when back

### Incremental Breadcrumbs
- Per-device latest-timestamp tracking via BreadcrumbAccumulator
- Only fetch positions newer than last known per device
- Deduplication by (device_id, timestamp)
- Max 100K positions in memory, FIFO eviction
- Reset on new mission, preserved on pause/resume

## Decisions (locked)
- HTTP polling for v1 (decided during spike research)
- Polling interval: 30s default, configurable
- Mock auth for tests; real credentials from Eamonn when available

## Dependencies
- M2 complete (map must be rendering)
- M3 complete (persistence for storing positions)

## Spike reference
- spikes/S7-traccar-integration/ — TraccarClient, PollingManager
- Investigation output: /tmp/m4-tracking.md

comments.author: Donal
comments.text: Readiness review: Research required = Medium. Implementation readiness = 4/5. Recommended action: implement after a short design pass. Locked enough to start: HTTP polling for v1, 30s default polling, deterministic device colours, incremental breadcrumb fetching, stale/offline semantics, last-good cache requirement, SQLite persistence via MissionStore. Open questions needing confirmation/research: 1) exact Traccar auth/session approach for production (basic auth vs token vs cookie/session handling, SSL expectations, failure modes), 2) exact operator-facing offline/degraded UI location and wording, 3) paused mission behavior boundary: bead says tracking data still accepted while auto-refresh is suppressed and manual refresh works, so we should confirm whether background polling fully stops during pause or continues but is hidden/suppressed in UI, 4) exact cache file ownership/location/retention policy if we want parity with the plugin. comments.author: Donal
comments.text:
Implemented and production-validated.

Root cause:
- The live Traccar server currently has no breadcrumb history in a 5h window, but does return history at larger windows.

title: M5: Mission UI — start/pause/resume/finish + timers
description:
Mission lifecycle controls and dual timer display.

## Deliverable
- Mission control panel: Start, Pause/Resume, Finish buttons (colour-coded, flashing when paused)
- Dual timers: Elapsed (total since start) + Active Search (excludes pauses)
- Mission name input on start
- Start offset: back-date mission start 0-48 hours
- Finish confirmation dialog — data remains editable after finish
- Auto-detect paused mission on restart → prompt Resume or Start Fresh
- Mission state persisted to SQLite

## NOT in M5 scope (deferred to Phase 3)
- Finalize (archive + lock read-only)
- Admin unlock
- Focus mode

## Lifecycle Model (canonical — resolves all contradictions)

### Persisted statuses in SQLite
`active`, `paused`, `finished`, `finalized` — these are real database values in the missions table.
`idle` is NOT a persisted status — it means 'no active mission' and is represented by the absence of an active/paused record.

### State machine (UI layer)
IDLE → ACTIVE → PAUSED ↔ ACTIVE → FINISHED
- IDLE is the app-shell state when no mission is active
- FINISHED is a terminal persisted state (unlike the plugin where it was transient)
- The mission record stays `finished` in the database until explicitly finalized (Phase 3)
- The UI resets to idle-like behaviour after finish (start button enabled, timers stopped)

### Valid transitions
- IDLE → ACTIVE (start_mission)
- ACTIVE → PAUSED (pause_mission)
- PAUSED → ACTIVE (resume_mission)
- ACTIVE → FINISHED (finish_mission)
- PAUSED → FINISHED (finish_mission while paused)
- No backwards from FINISHED. Must start a new mission.

## Pause Behaviour
- Active search timer **stops**. Elapsed timer **keeps running**.
- Tracking polling timer keeps ticking but **suppresses the actual refresh call**. No new data fetched.
- No background data accumulation while paused.
- Markers and drawings CAN still be placed/edited.
- Mission is still considered 'running' for purposes like breadcrumb filtering.
- UI: Pause button becomes Resume with flashing animation.

## Finish Behaviour
- Stops all timing.
- Mission record in SQLite set to `finished`.
- UI resets: Start enabled, Pause/Finish disabled.
- Data remains **editable** — not read-only.
- Tracking auto-refresh stops. Manual review of existing data still possible.
- Saved pause state cleared.
- Breadcrumb accumulator preserved until next start_mission.

## Start Fresh (on restart with paused mission)
- Dismisses the paused mission prompt.
- Clears the saved pause state from SQLite.
- Does NOT delete or archive the old mission data — it remains on disk.
- User must then start a new mission manually.
- If user enters the same mission name as an existing mission, warn (don't silently overwrite).

## Back-dated Start
- Range: 0-48 hours via spinner/input.
- Start time validated: must be timezone-aware UTC, must not be in the future.
- Breadcrumbs fetched from the back-dated time.
- Use case: teams already moving before command post starts, or replaying test data.

## Single Active Mission
- One active mission at a time.
- Starting new mission while paused is blocked — must resume or finish first.
- Multiple finished missions can coexist on disk.

## Timer Formulas (exact)
```
elapsed = max(0, now - mission_start_ts)
```
Where mission_start_ts respects the back-dated offset.

```
total_paused = sum of all completed pause durations + current ongoing pause (if any)
active = max(0, elapsed - total_paused)
```

On each resume: `paused_total += (now - pause_started_at)`
On finish while paused: same accumulation before stopping.

### Example
| Event | Time | paused_total | active |
|---|---|---|---|
| Start | T+0 | 0 | 0 |
| Pause | T+300 | 0 | 300 |
| Resume | T+600 | 300 | 300 |
| Pause | T+1200 | 300 | 900 |
| Resume | T+1500 | 600 | 900 |
| Finish | T+2000 | 600 | 1400 |

## Recovery on Restart
1. On app startup: query SQLite for mission with status = `active` or `paused`
2. If found with `paused`: show Resume/Start Fresh dialog
3. If found with `active` (crash during active mission): treat as paused, show same dialog
4. Resume: restore mission context, timers recalculated from persisted timestamps
5. Start Fresh: clear the mission's pause state, leave data on disk, return to idle

## Operator Dialog Copy (from plugin)

### Resume/Start Fresh prompt
Title: 'Resume Mission?'
Body: 'Found paused mission:\n\nMission: {name}\nStarted: {start_time}\n\nDo you want to resume this mission?'
Buttons: 'Resume Mission' (default) | 'Start Fresh'

### Finish confirmation
Title: 'End Mission'
Body: 'Are you sure you want to end this mission?\n\nThis will:\n• Stop mission timers\n• Keep all mission data editable\n• Reset UI for the next mission\n\nMission data remains saved.\nUse Finalize Mission later to archive and lock the data.'
Buttons: 'Yes' | 'No' (default No)

## Architecture
- Mission state machine lives in a dedicated domain module (not in UI components or persistence adapters)
- UI components subscribe to state changes via Zustand store
- Persistence writes happen through MissionStore Tauri commands
- Timer logic is pure functions, testable without UI

## Dependencies
- M2 complete ✅
- M3 complete ✅

## Evidence
- Plugin investigation: /tmp/m5-lifecycle.md, /tmp/m5-deep.md
- Plugin source: mission_controller.py, mission_lifecycle_controller.py, sar_panel.py

title: Active mission may transition to finished on app quit (desktop)
description:
Discovered during B3 (sartracker-web-ppr) first internal Tauri smoke build. Steps to reproduce on commit e7ead2eb093a, build 0.1.0+sha.e7ead2eb093a, packaged via 'npm run tauri build -- --bundles app':

1. Start a new mission (mission name 'Fdsfdsf', start time backdated 48h).
2. Configure Traccar (provider URL http://kmrtsar.ddns.net:8082, basic auth, apiuser/apiuser, auto-connect on).
3. Export diagnostics. Diagnostics confirms phase: active, current mission: Fdsfdsf.
4. Quit the app via osascript -e 'tell application "sartracker-web" to quit' (equivalent to Cmd+Q).
5. Relaunch the .app.

Expected: Active mission Fdsfdsf is recovered; mast shows the active mission still ticking, or the recovery prompt offers to resume.

Actual: After restart the mast reads 'No active mission / READY TO START'. Direct SQLite query against /Users/donalocallaghan/Library/Application Support/ie.kmrt.sartrackerweb/mission-store.sqlite shows mission status='finished' with finish_time 2026-05-17T07:06:50, ~7 seconds after the last 30s autosave backup, and the mission_events audit trail has no mission_paused or other user lifecycle event before mission_finished. The mission_finished row appears to coincide with the quit signal.

Two consistent hypotheses to disambiguate:
(a) before_quit / shutdown handler is invoking finish_mission instead of merely stopping autosave/runtime. Active missions become finished on every clean quit. Life-safety regression.
(b) An earlier UI interaction finished the mission and the audit row only landed at WAL flush time near quit. Less consistent with timestamps, but possible.

Evidence captured: tmp/beta-artifacts/smoke/01-04*.png, tmp/beta-artifacts/smoke/diagnostics-report-2026-05-17T07-05-55-676Z.txt (also saved by the user under their app data dir at the same name), and the mission_events query above. SQLite is on schema_version=4 with backup mirror healthy (mission-store.backup.sqlite at 144 KB).

Likely entry points to inspect:
- src-tauri/src/main.rs and src-tauri/src/lib.rs for tauri::Builder shutdown / before_quit / on_window_event hooks.
- src-tauri/src/persistence/* for finish_mission and any close-time path that may call it.
- src/lib/app-runtime-controller.ts and src/features/mission/** for any unmount/teardown that calls a finish API.
- src/infrastructure/mission-store.ts (renderer side) for any finish call wired to window unload.

Suggested triage steps:
1. Disable Traccar auto-connect and reproduce. If the bug persists with no provider, it is a pure lifecycle/teardown bug, not a tracking-runtime side effect.
2. Add a debug log on every code path that issues finish_mission and reproduce the quit.
3. Replay the quit while watching the mission_events table in a tail loop to time-correlate the row insert against the quit signal.
4. Add a regression test in src-tauri persistence tests covering 'quitting an app with an active mission must not transition the mission to finished'.

Severity: critical. This blocks promoting the 0.1.0 desktop beta. Item 4 (mission persists across restart) of the B2/B3 smoke checklist cannot pass while this exists.
close_reason: False positive. No code path automatically transitions an active mission to finished. Rust persistence regression test added to pin the contract; live test on packaged 0.1.0 .app confirms a quit signal leaves an active mission active. Closed.

comments.author: Donal
comments.text:
VERDICT: not a real bug. The B3 hypothesis was wrong.

Categorical proof (4 layers):

1. Code-path audit (subagent): no before_quit / RunEvent::ExitRequested / window-close handler exists in src-tauri/src/lib.rs or main.rs. Every callsite of finishMission() in src/ traces back to an explicit operator UI action (handleConfirmFinish on Mission Control, or controller.startFresh() from the recovery prompt's Start Fresh button). No automatic finish path exists.

2. Rust persistence regression test (NEW, in 000f7d1): active_mission_survives_process_restart_without_explicit_finish. Drops the MissionStore (process-exit equivalent), reopens the same database, asserts the mission is still status=Active and no mission_finished event was emitted. Passes.

3. Live live test on the post-fix packaged app: seeded an active mission directly into SQLite, launched the .app, app correctly auto-paused the mission for the recovery flow (audit row mission_paused, expected by start-mission-runtime.ts:46-48), kill -TERM'd the app. SQLite afterwards: mission status='paused', finish_time NULL, no mission_finished event ever appended. Mission survived the kill cleanly.

4. Final live test post-Resume: same packaged app, mission resumed to active, tracking ran and persisted 18 devices/14 positions, then quit via osascript. After quit: mission status='active', finish_time NULL, audit log contains only device_updated and mission_backup_synced events plus the original mission_resumed. No mission_finished.

Re-reading the original 2026-05-17 audit trail with this knowledge: the mission_finished row at 07:06:50 UTC for 'Fdsfdsf' was operator-driven (the user pressing Confirm Finish) ~41 seconds before my osascript quit. The earlier B3 report misattributed that to the quit signal.

Closing as resolved without further code changes — the regression test now pins the contract.

title: Mast tracking ratio 'positions/stale' is visually ambiguous
description:
The command mast tracking pill currently displays the value `${positions.length}/${staleCount}` under the dynamic label `${trackingStatus.mode}` (e.g. ONLINE). With 14 cached positions and 13 stale, this renders as "ONLINE 14/13", which scans visually as "14 of 13" — an impossible ratio.

Source: src/App.tsx:428
```ts
<TopReadout
  label={trackingStatus.mode}
  tone={trackingStatus.mode === 'online' ? 'success' : staleCount > 0 ? 'warning' : 'default'}
  value={`${snapshot.positions.length}/${staleCount}`}
/>
```

Discovered during deep production validation pass on 2026-05-17 against build `0.1.0+SHA.840323E34498` at https://sartracker-web.vercel.app/?missionHarness=1 with the standard apiuser/apiuser hosted Traccar proxy. Mast read `DEVICES 18 / ONLINE 14/13` while tracking-status panel read `DEVICES 18 / FIXES 14`.

Evidence: tmp/prod-validation-2026-05-17/03b-mast-after-tracking.png

This is operator-readability, not a functional bug — the underlying tracking polling, fixes count, and stale detection all work. But for a life-safety mast pill, "14/13" is exactly the kind of glance-readable signal that should not require interpretation.

Affects shared app (mast component is shared between hosted browser harness and Tauri), so the fix should land on master and be visible to both lanes.

Suggested investigation:
- Decide the right semantic: is this two independent values (positions, stale) or a ratio?
- If two independent values: split into separate chips or use a clearer separator (e.g. `14 fixes · 13 stale`, or `14 (13 stale)`).
- If a ratio: the denominator should be the larger-or-equal value (e.g. `online/total` = `n/devices` not positions/stale).
- Update the TopReadout consumers consistently.
- Add a unit/snapshot test pinning the new format.

Non-goals for this bead:
- Redesigning the entire mast layout.
- Changing the device-count chip semantics.

comments.author: Donal
comments.text: Closed 2026-05-17. Replaced the ambiguous 'positions/stale' mast value with two separate stacked chips inside the same 64px column: an uppercased mode label (ONLINE/OFFLINE/IDLE) coloured by tone, then a FIX row and a STALE row, each with its own small label and numeric value. STALE is amber/warning when > 0, muted when 0. Implementation lives behind a pure selector at src/features/tracking/command-mast-tracking-readout.ts so the rule is unit-testable and the cell only renders. New tests: tests/unit/command-mast-tracking-readout.test.ts (8 tests, includes a regression that JSON-stringifying the output never contains the impossible '14/13' substring), tests/e2e/v1-regression.spec.ts adds two operator-facing regressions (active 3 fix / 1 stale; idle 0/0) that fail red if the cell text ever matches /\d+\s*\/\s*\d+/. New visual entry mast-tracking-cell-active captures the new layout under a stale-warning scenario; AI review PASS. Full local verification all green.

title: H3: Improve workspace CLOSE affordance
description: CLOSE button on overlays is small text in top-right corner. Enlarge button, add × icon, show visible Esc keyboard hint. Support clicking backdrop/dimmed area to close.
close_reason: WorkspaceHeader with × icon close button, Esc kbd hint, data-testid. Backdrop click to close via WorkspaceOverlay. All 4 workspaces refactored to shared components. Triple-validated.

comments.text: IMPLEMENTATION PROTOCOL: Before starting, load /ui-ux-pro-max skill for design rules, contrast ratios, spacing scales, and checklist validation. Use its Quick Reference sections to verify your changes meet accessibility and interaction standards.

comments.author: Donal
comments.text:
VERIFICATION PROTOCOL (required before closing this bead):
1. Run unit tests: npm run test — all must pass
2. Run standard E2E tests: npm run test:e2e — all must pass
3. Run visual E2E tests: npx playwright test --project=visual — all must pass
4. Visual validation: Use Chrome DevTools MCP to navigate the app at http://127.0.0.1:1420/?missionHarness=1 and take screenshots of every view affected by this change. Compare before/after. Verify: contrast ratios, spacing consistency, text readability, interactive states, and that no regressions were introduced in unrelated views.
5. Satellite basemap check: Switch to ESRI Satellite basemap and verify any map-overlay changes remain legible.
6. Update handoff/HANDOFF.md with what changed and what was verified.
